preamble: The transformation from a voiced aspirated (breathy voiced) dental stop to an alveolar flap is a process of lenition common in Indo-Aryan languages, where the stop closure is reduced to a brief tap.

examples.note: Common dialectal or fast-speech weakening of voiced dental aspirates into rhotic sounds.
While primarily retroflex, this illustrates the systematic flappability of voiced aspirates in the family.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Cardona, G., & Jain, D. K. (2003). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
